replies.msg:
hkger has the freedom to hold gathering and the right is protected by the Basic Law, so supposedly there is no such thing as "illegal gathering" in that sense. you may have read from some sources claiming the protests were not "approved" by the police force, but actually hkger doesn't need the police s approval to hold gathering. what the ordinance says is more like informing the police that a gathering is upcoming because they may have to make arrangements like detours for buses etc.

so the reason that there are so many "illegal gatherings" is that the police is abusing the power granted by the ordinance to reject all applications, which essentially force hkger to choose between shut up or go ahead and engage in "illegal protests"

the history of the ordinance is rather complex and difficult to explain if you are not familiar with HK history, but you may try google it if you would like to know more
